speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's so crazy. Talks Machina is every Tuesday evening at 7:00pm Pacific where our fantastic, recently-escaped-from-the-Far-Realm Brian W. Foster will be hosting. This is discussed next week on the things that happened in this week's episode you're about to see. So check it out. It should be fun. I will be at A-Kon, June 8th to the 10th. So in a couple weeks, I'll be out there at Dallas/Fort Worth for panels, signings and going to talk and hang out with people as much as I can. So if you're in that area, come say hello! I believe--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So crazy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh, so sexy. He's a right nice one. But no. You managed to interrogate him, made him an offer he couldn't refuse and he agreed to lead you onto where the safe house exists within the swamp. You rented out the rooms in the subterranean chambers of the old and almost collapsed pub. You separated yourselves for the evening, completed your rest and we pick up as morning comes to greet you. Lightless, as there are no windows and you just eventually come to on what you assume is an evening's full rest. You all independently come to shake your heads and get yourselves prepared for what the next day's journey arises, you gather your things and you glare at the rather awkward Febron across the way, who, as you wake up, is just sitting there watching you sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's so sexy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh, so sexy. He's a right nice one. But no. You managed to interrogate him, made him an offer he couldn't refuse and he agreed to lead you onto where the safe house exists within the swamp. You rented out the rooms in the subterranean chambers of the old and almost collapsed pub. You separated yourselves for the evening, completed your rest and we pick up as morning comes to greet you. Lightless, as there are no windows and you just eventually come to on what you assume is an evening's full rest. You all independently come to shake your heads and get yourselves prepared for what the next day's journey arises, you gather your things and you glare at the rather awkward Febron across the way, who, as you wake up, is just sitting there watching you sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's so sexy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh, so sexy. He's a right nice one. But no. You managed to interrogate him, made him an offer he couldn't refuse and he agreed to lead you onto where the safe house exists within the swamp. You rented out the rooms in the subterranean chambers of the old and almost collapsed pub. You separated yourselves for the evening, completed your rest and we pick up as morning comes to greet you. Lightless, as there are no windows and you just eventually come to on what you assume is an evening's full rest. You all independently come to shake your heads and get yourselves prepared for what the next day's journey arises, you gather your things and you glare at the rather awkward Febron across the way, who, as you wake up, is just sitting there watching you sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's so sexy.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Right. Coming right up! Go an find a place to sit, be with you in about an hour."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You don't sleep with the daggers, Kiri. You want to remove them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chirping)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should have taught her that last night.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's pretty-- I'm glad we're alive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys all begin to gather and make your way up the ladder, through the hatch in the floor of the Keystone Pub, which is not bustling by any means. There are maybe three patrons in the process of having their morning ale. You glance over the side and you can see Dent Bonswallow who's in the process of cleaning off a number of the glasses from the night before. He looks over in your direction and goes, "Oh, right. Bright morning to you all. Rise up! Would you like breakfast of some kind, hey?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot of fucking energy this early in the morning. God. (clears throat)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I would like so much of whatever you have, please, thank you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You gather your things, finish off the remainder of your lackluster breakfast. At this point now, the morning air is chilled, though there is still humidity that permeates the swamp vicinity. You hear the bugs beginning to grow louder with the distant cicada/cricket sounds in the distance echoing through the swamp. There is a low fog that is fairly prominent this early in the morning. You can see about 15 or 20 feet beyond the barrier of the outer swamp before the moisture begins to pick up. You see just the shaded trunks of trees beyond that for another 60 or so feet before it becomes completely obscured by fog.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Here you go. You can sign this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Okay! Sorry. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, we have a deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good, you're in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Welcome to The Mighty Nein!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (like Jester) "Welcome to The Mighty Nein!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: This bird is adorable!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit there's not-- no, we're not ironic any more!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, we'll see how we are at the end of the day. Bet that'll fix itself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we are. Because when we write the name down, it's actually the Zemnian word for no. So even if there's nine people, then--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're overthinking it Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a double entendre.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we haven't eaten-- oh yes, we have. Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lots of pocket bacon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We ordered coffee? We're still waiting on coffee? Is there going to be a desert? Was there a desert menu? Can I get a container? Eggs never keep, anyway.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You take a moment to scout the vicinity. At this point in the morning, it's maybe about nine o'clock or so judging by where you can see the source of sunlight in the somewhat clouded sky. Glancing around the city, not a lot of folks are moving. It seems like the nature of the swamp town is a little bit of a slower pace of living by the nature of the location. The few folks that are wandering around the streets are slowly getting to their daily chores and business. No one seems to be particularly interested. A few folks look over because you are a gathered motley crew on the edge of town, but there's no one who seems to be very focused in what's going on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit! Kiri, come back, Kiri.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Kiri come back!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Runs back, feet leaving a trail behind as she's stepping through two to three inches of swamp water across this district. (chirping)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You have to be careful. There's lots of creatures around that could hurt you, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There's lots of creatures around that could hurt you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You always want to stick together. You never want to split the party.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, stay close. Stay close to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She gets right behind Yasha and sticks right to her back with the dagger out on the opposite side going--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should really give her like a sheath or something to put that knife away, Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think you tell kids to run with scissors, she'll be fine. Can we check to make sure nobody's following us as we're heading out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Plus four, 16.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Actually, you both rolled pretty high. Over the next two hours or so, you guys manage to keep for the most part above any of the deeper recesses of marsh water. Leaping from cluster of tree base and sections of ground that are mostly above the water line, it manages to go through without much of an issue. The fog does slowly begin to burn off as the hours progress, but it's still present. You gather probably about the time you arrived at the end of day yesterday to the exterior of the Labenda Swamp, there was just a faint bit of fog at the ground. It will be a number of hours before you even reach that point, but it is progressing towards a more visible space. These two hours you guys continue forward, being mindful and watching around. Those who are keeping watch, I need both of you to make perception checks or one of you make it with advantage, your choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was a joke.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah, gotcha

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said, "Hi, Beaus."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah, boughs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know. But I said "Hi, Beaus."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Three out of five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's fine. In pushing this forward and guiding the path, I'm going to have Febron and whoever is keeping watch on the exterior to both make survival checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean on the exterior?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm keeping watch on the right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm keeping watch up front.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking on the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The gators come from the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right. Well this isn't a perception check, this is to make their way through the swamp to keep track of what's ahead, so I'm having Febron and one of you take point on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What kind of a check is this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Survival.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Oh, I thought it was actually higher when I first looked at it. 17.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Actually, you both rolled pretty high. Over the next two hours or so, you guys manage to keep for the most part above any of the deeper recesses of marsh water. Leaping from cluster of tree base and sections of ground that are mostly above the water line, it manages to go through without much of an issue. The fog does slowly begin to burn off as the hours progress, but it's still present. You gather probably about the time you arrived at the end of day yesterday to the exterior of the Labenda Swamp, there was just a faint bit of fog at the ground. It will be a number of hours before you even reach that point, but it is progressing towards a more visible space. These two hours you guys continue forward, being mindful and watching around. Those who are keeping watch, I need both of you to make perception checks or one of you make it with advantage, your choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was a joke.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah, gotcha

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said, "Hi, Beaus."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah, boughs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know. But I said "Hi, Beaus."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Three out of five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's fine. In pushing this forward and guiding the path, I'm going to have Febron and whoever is keeping watch on the exterior to both make survival checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean on the exterior?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm keeping watch on the right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm keeping watch up front.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking on the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The gators come from the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right. Well this isn't a perception check, this is to make their way through the swamp to keep track of what's ahead, so I'm having Febron and one of you take point on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What kind of a check is this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Survival.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Oh, I thought it was actually higher when I first looked at it. 17.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yeah, we don't usually leave this early in the morning."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to make it with advantage or separate?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nah. One.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's not great for me either. Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, jeez Louise.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Clear on the right!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Clear on the front!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, front--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (shouting) Clear on the left? Man, which way am I looking?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's really reassuring when there's a question mark on the end of your statement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is generally how it goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Febron at this point's still looking. He says, "Wait, hold on a second." He starts doing a little circle in the area, checking out some of the recent trees, looking at the sun in the sky and he's like, "Yeah. I feel like we're going the right way."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wait, just to be clear  Your job-- your only job was to walk to a building and back for god knows how many years and you think we're going the right way?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Well, this one they're doing it individually, because you have somebody outside of-- Along the path as you guys are progressing for the next hour or so, Febron and Beau are keeping pace on this. Keeping watch, moving forward, you step through some of the soggier areas of the swamp and your foot gets stuck a bit and it's starting to get a little more difficult to traverse. Febron's like, "It's just a few more hours and we'll make our way to the outskirts of the lake. It'll be a lot easier then, trust me. It'll be fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Febron, do you have any cups? I don't want you to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He pulls out a half-filled wineskin, opens it up, pours the water out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Have some booze. I'll fill it to the brim.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks curiously at the flask and the amount of liquid you poured in there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Cheers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Cheers indeed. Tiny girl." He watches as you drink the alcohol and is confused. Gets a closer look at you at this point and he goes--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Now is not the time to start making judgments, sir.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does that clear your memory?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Aye. It's this way." Make another survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Mr. Fjord, you're very liberal with your parenting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't fuck me, Sunny.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's just milk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Oh. That makes sense.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Man, that's really frustrating. Seven.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Did you both roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Huh?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, it was Febron and Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Seven. Not good. I keep getting so close.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do we not get advantage?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you the better part of a minute to finally shake him out of it, before he goes, "Oh, is it-- what? Shit! (yelling) Shit, what's going on?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He continues to walk towards it, and then glides up in the air and is floating. He's reaching towards it, and the plant (slurping) and is trying to reach for him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take my staff and bat him away like a helium balloon. Just start batting him back over away from it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's now floating and slowly rotating in the air, like (grunting).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: I'll be raising him up and down, shaking him in the air, like, "Come on, shake out of it, come on!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we try to outmaneuver this one now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I think we've already had our fun with the one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I open my journal and start drawing him as a balloon.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hold on. We're not done.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, there's a lot happening over there.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I need you and probably Beau, since you were with Febron before, make a perception check, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's that way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "(stuttering) Not north, where we're going!" At this point, you guys can see the very edge of the water is now starting to show through the fog and you can see where bits of the lake are hitting the shoreline, and you have reached the Ounterloch in the middle of the Labenda Swamp.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, there's a lake!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: With that, life delivers a pleasant surprise.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Febron.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there monsters in this lake?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know, I don't go into it. I recommend not getting too close, just in case. We usually stay right on the coastal side of it, you know?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perfect."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (splashing)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, what're you doing?! No, shit!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then I go back out. I just wanted to see.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The whole time you're doing that, Kiri's like (chirping). Who's keeping watch on this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Keeping watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You take the handful and lob them quietly, as far as you can. You hear very faint impacts of them about 25, 30 feet off. The trollish creature goes (snarling) and pushes off the rock a bit, looking that direction. Looks over the rock, looks back in that direction. It's now looking like he's paranoid and keeping tabs on both sides.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it a troll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It appears to be trollish in nature. The pustules and part of its body, there's this thick green liquid that's oozing out in places. It's sitting there clinging to the rock with its long fingers, clutching it, looking over in the direction of where you were, breathing heavy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he see The Mighty Nein?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It seems to be looking in the direction where you were.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But he doesn't see me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He doesn't see you at the moment, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Do not see me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How far away am I from him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're about 35 feet from it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Could I theoretically throw something behind him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You could certainly try.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, sure. I'm going to take out a handful of ball bearings and try and toss them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a sleight of hand check to do so without making a very visible toss.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, right? No sound at all.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so it is now the troll's turn. The troll does not regenerate because you did acid damage to it with your last attack, which prevents it from regaining any hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Woo, nice! Both parts of its back where they impact, the flesh tears open, revealing the muscle beneath, and as it does, more of it sprays. Beau--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit, right!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take five points of poison damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hang on, let me make sure I'm doing my math right, too. It was 33 points of damage is what you did before, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was 15 plus 18, so it's 33.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You just took another 20 points of poison damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I am unconscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What?! Way to go, Mee-ster Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, don't anybody else hit it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, please don't pick up Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Beau falls onto the ground, arms limp, covered in this toxic substance. That ends your turn, Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I cast Regret. (laughing)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: I cast Major Regret at 5th-level.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's now Kiri's turn. Kiri holding a dagger--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You can use your str--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh I can use my strength!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It makes sense, and it's how we did it in the last campaign, too. It's one of the rules that they say you can use for your campaign. Charisma or strength for your intimidation because it makes sense for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes, okay! Okay, okay, okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: My favorite Pike line, by the way, in Pillars Of Eternity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was just thinking that as well!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay! 22!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 22, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I died when I heard that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, it turns around and glances at you and it looks shaken. I'll say, for the purposes of this, it's frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's going to keep running away from you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's all happening like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does that finish your turn, Yasha?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I like slap myself?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Unfortunately--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Does that not work? Shit. Wasted a rage.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so when we come to your turn, you can do that. You come to consciousness, and you're like (panting) and your body hurts, there's all this stinging from the toxin burning your skin and flesh. You're chucked under and you can feel the claws digging into part of your thigh and side as you're being run along by this giant troll creature. It smells awful; this heavy scent of rotting citrus and fungus that's permeating from its body. That finishes your turn, Jester?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's all happening like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does that finish your turn, Yasha?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I like slap myself?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Unfortunately--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Does that not work? Shit. Wasted a rage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's now Kiri's turn. Kiri holding a dagger--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kiri be my savior!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Aw Kiri.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She just holds the dagger. That's Kiri's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Kiri.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And shouts, (like Jester) "Fuck!" Jester, it's your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get within 60 feet of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you're within 60 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Awesome! I'm going to cast Healing Word on Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As my bonus action. Healing Word, so you regain 1d4 plus four hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Seven points!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you are conscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! I'm conscious!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Being grappled by a troll, one thing at a time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Seven hit points, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Modern Literature!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Lie down, play dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We don't know what that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to wake up, see my situation and go (gasps) and play dead.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That finishes your turn. It's now the troll's turn. It gets up, half its movement, and it's going to run 15 feet this way and then a full action run for another 30 feet, and it's darting off with the corpse of Febron under its arm. It has its prize and it's running off into the swamp. It's hurting, but you're watching its flesh stitch together a little more and starting to heal up from some of the wounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have a bonus, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But I can't do a range.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's only unarmed. I think that's your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Glug glug.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Glug glug?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't have a healing potion.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She doesn't have a glug glug.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll move five feet closer towards el troll, and I'll say: Get the fuck out of here! And I'll Eldritch Blast twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it. Disadvantage on each strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's going to miss anyway, and the second one. They both miss.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They both go passing right over its body as it's trying to get up. It slips for a second, and what would've hit, it ducks out of the way just in time and both of them arc off, hitting nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You get your ass out of this forest!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The few words you make out involve a time. One of them says two hours past the noon sun, that's the most you make out of one sentence. Part of it's smeared. It's like Steve Martin in The Jerk trying to read the love letter. (gibberish) The other things you can make out are four men required, and then another one saying something about Shady-- and that's all you make out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (hiccups)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (imitates a hiccup)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's cursed hiccups.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And a note that's keeping track of locations, but it's mostly soaked now and the ink has all run, and you make out bits of words here and there, but it makes no sense to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm taking it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Give me the charm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you going to check if it's magic?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't check if it's magic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use Mending on the note. Oh wait, it doesn't make the words come back. Remember, we already figured this part out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the paper is now dry and a full piece of paper, but the ink is still as smeared and lost as it was before.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I look at the words?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Indeed. Bringing us back into the game. You guys have taken your short rest, healed up a bit, and recovered from the chaos of the swamp you managed to trudge through. You've lost your guide, but you've survived thus far. The rest of you, at least. What would you like to do? Oh, you were casting--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello everyone, and welcome back! I just dropped my note. I'll pick it up in a second. So before we get into it, we're going to announce the winner of our giveaway! The winner of our fantastic Wyrmwood giveaway is the_bowmeister. (surfer voice) The_bowmeister, enjoy your Wyrmwood! (normal voice) So congratulations, we'll get that sent out to you ASAP.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's very Wildemount, the_bowmeister.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The_bowmeister? Ja, possibly would be. Back into the game we go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, now you got it on Mark!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ugh! (blood spraying)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Roll a d4.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just real quick, I wanted to give a quick shoutout to my brother-in-law, Peter Habib of Mr. Fantastic Music. They did the track for the animated thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So good!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So good! I love it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (Scottish accent) I love it!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You left early enough in the day where you aren't threatened for dusk for another few hours. You guys continue on past the tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Four men required.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You, my good friend, would you walk with me in the direction that the dead person said that this entrance is in? Before my magic runs out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you feel the need to call me a good friend?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're right. Person that I work with, would you walk over before my spell runs out so that I can use it to help the group?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why don't we all head in that direction, maybe? In a large, well knit group.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Since there are things that'll be snagging bitches when we get separated.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Is it dark, or is it--?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, at this point it's later afternoon, maybe. It's like two or three o'clock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe like, two past noon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Maybe.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb knows exactly what time it is. Liam likes to burn his friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb also likes to burn his friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Aw.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (singing) One thing, we've got it coming.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You look up. The ceiling's about 12 feet tall and there's some hanging roots that are dangling from the top. It looks like it's been carved away maybe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't see anything inside here. Looks like there was a broken lamp, so careful with fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: There's got to be more here, right? This can't be it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'll push in a little bit more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You're now stepping into the chamber. The chamber's about 30 feet wide. It's just round.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Looking up?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There are some spatters of blood and there are some things that give the sign of being pulled on as well as pieces and shards of wood, including bits of porcelain, some object that had shattered and broken. Looks like whatever may have been within this chamber at some point, a lot of it's been brought down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are there any immediate threats in there, Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not that I can see.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, as you're glancing about answering this question, you look down on the far north corner of it and where some of the roots have gathered and come down, there's one space where you don't see the wall of the tree behind it. As you move the root out of the way, there is an actual hole about two and a half feet wide that descends below the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not any danger in the chamber, but it looks like there's more to this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like they were dragging bodies down there? The blood streaks and stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could've been.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Do they lead there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, do the tracks lead to the hole?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The hole has chunks of rock and dirt as well as a very interwoven network of roots coming down from the tree, so it's fairly easy to climb down. There is a little bit of that damp water and moisture that seeped into the chamber previously from just being near the swamp. It wasn't a thick layer of water, maybe half an inch or so in the actual portions of the chamber and some of it is now heading down. As you're climbing down carefully through this hole, you can feel it running down your back and it's this cold swamp water. I need you to make an athletics check as you make your way down this chamber.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do you go back and forth?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, only one place to go and that's down.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait! I'm just going to take a quick minute to stare into the eternal abyss of the dodecahedron.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I just open up the bag so you can see it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god what is in there?!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We don't let Cali see it. Sorry, Cali.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You take a moment to focus and place your hand on this lightly thrumming device. Your vision is pulled into this universal space of stars and gas and distant existences you've never contemplated and there you see before you the pulsing light of possibility. You reach out a hand and grasp it and feel it enter your body and you've acquired your fragment of possibility.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't take much to distract Cali. You just point over there and she's like--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll take a swig and go down the hole.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Got it, cool. That ends your turn? It is now their turn. So, top of the round, they're both in the space, so they take how much damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You had to do that on your turn. You've only got an action you were holding. Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How high's the ceiling here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's about ten, 11 feet. It's not very high.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I would have had to have said it earlier, which I didn't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, that ends Fjord and Beau's turn. Molly?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Where am I? Let's see. I'm over there? I'm going to run as far as I can go towards Fjord, and then I'm going to spend the rest of my action running across and get into that little alcove.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This one over here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, the other side of it, I'm so sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, like behind that little--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I see. There we go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So positioning and ready for things to come.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You still have a bonus, if you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've got nothing for my bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Did you activate one of your scimitars?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: One is activated, the other is not.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You got it. It's now their turn. Top of the turn, they take 2d6 damage, so go ahead and roll that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure. All right, that ends your turn. Fjord, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to hug this wall if I can, back up a little bit, and I'm going to hold Eldritch Blast if I see them emerge from the darkness.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that ends your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did I have any more movement, by the by?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You did have a little bit more, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Could I have scooched up a bit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You could have, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just a scooch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm scooching!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can scoot all the way up to here, if you want.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've got very long legs right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're squat-moving your way through the cavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: A core stance is very good for your abs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Molly, what do you got?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There ain't nothing for me to hit, so I'm going to slightly peek out around the corner, so that I can keep an eye on things, and I'm going to hold my attack for anything that comes within range.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No, short rest you can do as many times as you want, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will join him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Anyone else feel like going for a swim?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I could probably join you for a swim.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: I need to check and make sure it's not here, and if there is a room back there I need to make sure it's not here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We don't know if there's going to be air on the other side of that, though, so we need people that can--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, one person that can breathe really well. Fjord, you are familiar with the sea. Can you hold your breath for a long time?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, pretty decent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right. How about you go--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Like, forever?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: As long as you don't mind dying, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have gills?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What if we tie a rope to you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure, we have an extra 50 feet, we didn't really need the rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we take a short rest again? Oh, it's another hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're going to do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you can only do it once a day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What, the--

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Short rest.
